medicalArticleRecord.title: Datopotamab deruxtecan in patients with untreated, advanced triple-negative breast cancer (TROPION-Breast02): a randomised, open-label, international, phase III trial
medicalArticleRecord.creator: Dent R, Shao Z, Schmid P, Cortes J, Cescon DW, Saji S, Jung KH, Bachelot T, Wang S, Ramírez EM, Basaran G, Stradella A, Mathiba R, Chen SC, Shen K, Wéber Á, Battelli N, Niikura N, Luo T, Chae YS, Fischbach N, Garbaos G, Patera A, Zhao K, Vuković P, Maxwell MJ, Traina T; TROPION-Breast02 investigators
medicalArticleRecord.abstract: Patients with untreated advanced triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) who were not candidates for immunotherapy were randomly assigned to receive datopotamab deruxtecan (Dato-DXd) or standard chemotherapy in the phase III TROPION-Breast02 trial. Among 644 patients, Dato-DXd significantly improved progression-free survival (10.8 vs. 5.6 months) and overall survival (23.7 vs. 18.7 months) compared with chemotherapy. Treatment-related adverse events were manageable, and no treatment-related deaths occurred. These findings support Dato-DXd as an effective first-line treatment option for patients with advanced TNBC who are ineligible for immunotherapy.
medicalArticleRecord.citation: Ann Oncol. 2026 Apr 3:S0923-7534(26)00130-4. doi: 10.1016/j.annonc.2026.03.008. Online ahead of print.
medicalArticleRecord.population: Patients with untreated advanced triple-negative breast cancer (TNBC) ineligible for immunotherapy
medicalArticleRecord.outcome: Improved progression-free survival and overall survival compared with chemotherapy
medicalArticleRecord.affiliation: Department of Medical Oncology, National Cancer Center Singapore
